input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Agent role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

Pipeline position: after summary1 and before second-hop retrieval. Inputs are the original question and the first-hop intermediate state. Functional responsibility: produce one focused retrieval query that is likely to recover the missing evidence needed by the downstream agents. Query quality is determined by evidence recovery, not by whether the query itself resembles or contains the final answer.

Native output contract: The module must consume `question` and `summary_1` and produce exactly one compact second-hop query field named `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include every distinct entity token visible in the inputs in the single terse query and place at most one neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the targeted relation or attribute (for example: "EntityA EntityB director").
- If a visible disambiguator or attribute token appears adjacent to an entity in the inputs (for example a multiword service name, an explicit page title, a year, or an attribute marker like a birth/producer/service/series cue), include that disambiguator token together with the associated entity in the query rather than dropping or truncating it.
- If `summary_1` contains an explicit page-title token sequence (for example an instruction-like ExactPageTitle or Fetch page title), prefer that exact page-title token sequence as the query value.
- When the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.

retrieved_doc_snippets: List of Johnny Bravo episodes | "Johnny Bravo" is an American animated television series created by Van Partible for the Cartoon Network. The series premiered July 14, 1997, and ended officially August 27, 2004. The series is a spin-off of "World Premiere Toons", also known as the "What a Cartoon! Show".

Johnny Bravo | Johnny Bravo is an American animated television series created by Van Partible for Cartoon Network, and the second of the network's Cartoon Cartoons. The series centers on the title character, a muscular and boorish young man who tries to get women to date him, though he is usually unsuccessful. He ends up in bizarre situations and predicaments, often accompanied by celebrity guest characters such as Donny Osmond or Adam West. Throughout its run, the show was controversial for its ...[truncated]

Van Partible | Van Partible (born Efrem Giovanni Bravo Partible on December 13, 1971) is a Filipino-born American animator, writer and producer best known for creating the animated television series "Johnny Bravo".

Cartoon Network: Block Party | Cartoon Network: Block Party is an arcade-style video game for Game Boy Advance developed by Majesco Entertainment and released in the United States on August 5, 2004 and in Europe and Oceania on September 15, 2004. It features characters from Cartoon Network original animated series such as "Johnny Bravo", "Ed Edd n Eddy", "Courage the Cowardly Dog", and "Cow and Chicken".

Cartoon Network Racing | Cartoon Network Racing is a racing video game developed by Eutechnyx (PS2) and Firebrand Games (Nintendo DS version) and published by The Game Factory on December 4, 2006, in North America, and on February 9, 2007, in Europe. The gameplay is similar to Nintendo's 2003 game "", but the characters and racetracks are from Cartoon Network's original animated television series. The six series featured are "Courage the Cowardly Dog", "Cow and Chicken", "Dexter's Laboratory", " ...[truncated]

Cartoon Network Amazone Waterpark | Cartoon Network Amazone Waterpark (Thai: สวนน้ำการ์ตูนเน็ทเวิร์ค อเมโซน) is the world's first Cartoon Network themed waterpark located at 888 Moo 8, Na Jomtien, Sattahip, Chonburi, Thailand, 15 kilometers away from South Pattaya. The park has recreated an atmosphere of Amazon rainforest with several attractions and activities such as water slides, artificial sea, rafting, flowriding and live shows, all featuring Cartoon Network's famous characters including Be ...[truncated]

Johnny Test (season 5) | The fifth season of the American animated television series "Johnny Test" originally aired on Cartoon Network in the United States. The season was announced by Cookie Jar Entertainment on August 24, 2010, consisting of 26 episodes, with two segments each. In the United States, the season premiered on Cartoon Network on June 13, 2011.

Partible paternity | Partible paternity or shared paternity is a cultural conceptualization of paternity according to which a child is understood to have more than one father; for example, because of an ideology that sees pregnancy as the cumulative result of multiple acts of sexual intercourse. In societies with the concept of partible paternity this often results in the nurture of a child being shared by multiple fathers in a form of polyandric relation to the mother, although this is not alwa ...[truncated]

Seth MacFarlane filmography | Seth MacFarlane is an American actor, animator, writer, producer, director, comedian, and singer. MacFarlane began his career has an animator and writer for Hanna-Barbera for several television series, including "Johnny Bravo", "Cow and Chicken", "Dexter's Laboratory", "I Am Weasel", and created a sequel to his college thesis film "Larry & Steve".

Partible inheritance | Partible inheritance is a system of inheritance in which property is apportioned among heirs. It contrasts in particular with primogeniture (common in feudal society), which requires that the whole or most of the inheritance passes to the eldest son, and with agnatic seniority where the succession passes to next senior male.

Van Zant | Van Zant is an American musical duo composed of brothers Donnie Van Zant and Johnny Van Zant. Both are brothers of the late Ronnie Van Zant, former lead singer for the Southern rock band Lynyrd Skynyrd. Johnny became the lead vocalist for the reunited Lynyrd Skynyrd in 1987, and Donnie was also the leader and vocalist of .38 Special.
